zh.Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, England: 英國薩默塞特哈契 比奧甘
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Roger Seymour於1321年出生在威爾士蒙姆爾福時，他的父親Roger St Maur II37歲，母親Joan Dameral St Maur27歲。他於1347年8月29日之後與Cecily de Beauchamp在英國薩默塞特哈契 比奧甘結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於從1359年到1366年7月29日在英國薩默塞特哈契 比奧甘。

zh-hans.Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, England: 英国薩默塞特哈契 比奧甘
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Roger Seymour于1321年出生在威尔士蒙姆爾福时，他的父亲Roger St Maur II37岁，母亲Joan Dameral St Maur27岁。他于1347年8月29日之后与Cecily de Beauchamp在英国薩默塞特哈契 比奧甘结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于从1359年到1366年7月29日在英国薩默塞特哈契 比奧甘。

nameMeanings.name: Seymour
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (London): of Norman origin, a habitational name from Saint-Maur-des-Fossés (Seine), and possibly also from Saint-Maur-des-Bois (Manche) and Saint-Maur-sur-Loire (Touraine), named from the dedication of their churches to Saint Maur (see   Moore   3). The family of this name who have held the title Duke of Somerset since 1547 are said by some to have originated in Saint-Maur-des-Fossés (Seine), while others claim they are from Saint-Maur-sur-Loire (Touraine). They rose to prominence under the Tudors, when Edward Seymour (died 1552) held a high position of office under Henry VIII, who was married to Edward's sister, Jane. The Norman name has been established in Ireland since at least the 17th century.

variants.name: Moore
meanings: English: from Middle English more ‘moor, marsh, fen’ (Old English mōr), hence a topographic name for someone who lived in such a place, or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, as for example Moore in Cheshire or More in Shropshire.
English (of Norman origin): ethnic name from Old French more ‘Moor’, either someone from North Africa or, more often, a nickname for someone thought to resemble a Moor. Compare Morrell and Moreau .

English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus), originally denoting either ‘Moor’ or someone with a swarthy complexion (compare Morrell , Morrin , Morris , and sense 2 above). There was a 6th-century Christian saint of this name.

variants.name: Semar
meanings: Germanized form of French Saint Marc, the name of a Huguenot family that migrated to the Palatinate.
English: variant of   Seymour  . This surname is very rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name, Rog(i)er, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + gār, gēr ‘spear’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, replacing the native Old English form Hrōðgār. Roger was one of the most popular boys' names throughout the medieval period, but less so after the Reformation, though it has continued in regular use to the present day. Roger, Count of Sicily ( c.1031–1101 ), son of Tancred, recovered Sicily from the Arabs. His son, also called Roger, ruled Sicily as king, presiding over a court noted for its splendour and patronage of the arts.

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roger  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Roger Seymour was born in 1321, in Penhow, Monmouthshire, Wales, his father, Roger St Maur II, was 37 and his mother, Joan Dameral St Maur, was 27. He married Cecily de Beauchamp after 29 August 1347, in Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died from 1359 to 29 July 1366, in Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, England.

fr.Penhow, Monmouthshire, Wales, United Kin: Penhow, Monmouthshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Roger Seymour est né en 1321, à Penhow, Monmouthshire, Galles, son père, Roger St Maur II, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Joan Dameral St Maur, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Cecily de Beauchamp après 29 août 1347, à Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé du 1359 au 29 juillet 1366, à Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Roger Seymour 1321 in Penhow, Monmouthshire, Wales geboren wurde, war sein Vater Roger St Maur II 37 und seine Mutter Joan Dameral St Maur 27. Er hat nach 29. August 1347 in Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, England Cecily de Beauchamp geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb von 1359 bis 29. Juli 1366 in Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Roger Seymour è nato il nel 1321 nel Penhow, Monmouthshire, Galles, il suo padre, Roger St Maur II, aveva 37 anni e il suo madre, Joan Dameral St Maur, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Cecily de Beauchamp dopo il 29 agosto 1347, nel Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì da 1359 a 29 luglio 1366, nel Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Roger Seymourが1321年にウェールズモンモウスシュアイアペンhオウで生まれた時，父Roger St Maur IIは37歳，母Joan Dameral St Maurは27歳であった。1347年8月29日以後にイギリスソメルセトハトチ ビーウチャンップでCecily de Beauchampと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼はから1359年まで1366年7月29日にイギリスソメルセトハトチ ビーウチャンップで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Roger Seymour(이)가 1321년 웨일스 몬모우스실르 펜호에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Roger St Maur II(은)는 37세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Dameral St Maur(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1347년 8월 29일 이후 영국 서머세트 해트치 보챔프에서 Cecily de Beauchamp(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1359년 부터 1366년 7월 29일까지 영국 서머세트 해트치 보챔프에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Roger Seymour родился в 1321 г. в местности пеноу, монмоутшир, Уэльс, его отцу по имени Roger St Maur II было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Dameral St Maur, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Cecily de Beauchamp после 29 августа 1347 г. в местности хач бочамп, сомирсет, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер с 1359 по 29 июля 1366 в местности хач бочамп, сомирсет, Англия.
